title: Stop or redesign the use case
summary: If the system fits a prohibited practice, it should not be placed on the EU market or used unless a very specific legal exception applies.

title: Risk management system
summary: Implement a continuous process to identify, analyse, estimate and mitigate reasonably foreseeable risks throughout the system lifecycle.

title: Data governance and quality
summary: Document data practices, quality criteria, bias considerations and the suitability of training, validation and testing datasets where applicable.

title: Technical documentation
summary: Maintain technical documentation sufficient to demonstrate conformity, including intended purpose, design, data, metrics, evaluation and controls.

title: Automatic event logging
summary: Design or require logging capabilities that provide appropriate traceability for monitoring operation, incidents and downstream use.

title: Transparency and instructions for use
summary: Provide clear information on capabilities, limitations, expected performance, intended use and human oversight measures.

title: Human oversight
summary: Define controls so competent people can interpret outputs, intervene, disregard or stop the system when required.

title: Confirm Annex III scope
summary: Verify the specific Annex III category: employment, credit, education, essential services, critical infrastructure, biometrics or other regulated areas.

title: AI interaction notice
summary: Inform people when they interact with an AI system, chatbot, synthetic content or deepfake, subject to applicable exceptions.

title: GPAI obligations review
summary: If you use or develop general-purpose AI models, review whether systemic risk exists and whether GPAI provider obligations apply.

title: Deployer obligations
summary: Use the system according to instructions, ensure human oversight, keep logs where relevant, monitor operation and perform required assessments.

title: Provider or substantial modification obligations
summary: If you develop or substantially modify the system, prepare conformity, technical documentation, instructions, quality management and registration where applicable.

title: Good practice for minimal risk
summary: Even without specific high-risk obligations, keep an AI inventory, internal owner, supplier review and acceptable-use policy.
